article_title: OpenAI, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and Nvidia Publish MRC, an Ethernet-Based Networking Protocol Built for 100,000-GPU AI Clusters
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the MRC 1.0 / OCP release (PR #1193, merged earlier in this batch). The work is discarded under v3.9.0; the contributor does not get a rewrite pass.

findings.details: Already-published article: src/content/articles/2026-05/08-openai-amd-broadcom-intel-microsoft-and-nvidia-release-mrc-10-to-ocp-an-open-rdma-protocol-already-running-openais-largest-training-clusters.md (originally PR #1193). Same MRC 1.0 / OCP release, same six-company collaborator list, same Fairwater + Abilene production deployments, same May 6 2026 announcement date.

content_preview.title: OpenAI, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and Nvidia Publish MRC, an Ethernet-Based Networking Protocol Built for 100,000-GPU AI Clusters
content_preview.summary: Multipath Reliable Connection extends RDMA over Ethernet with multipath transfers and microsecond failure recovery, and has been contributed to the Open Compute Project.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and Nvidia have jointly published Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC), a new networking protocol designed to keep training jobs running across the kind of GPU clusters now being built for frontier AI models. The specification was contributed to the Open Compute Project, according to [Network World](https://www.networkworld.com/article/4168926/openai-led-consortium-seeks-to-address-ai-processing-bottlenecks.html) and [The Decoder](https://the-d...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written; uses Network World, The Decoder, and SiliconANGLE rather than the vendor + customer announcement pair PR #1193 used. On its own merits, would APPROVE.
editor_notes.source_verification: Sources reachable and reputable. Independent of the duplicate flag, content is verifiable.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same MRC 1.0 release facts as the already-published article — same protocol, same six-company collaborator list, same OCP release path, same Fairwater/Abilene production deployments.
concerns: Duplicate disclosure event coverage. Different source set (Network World/The Decoder/SiliconANGLE vs. NVIDIA+OpenAI vendor announcements), but same underlying news.
recommendations: Bot operations: parallel write-article agents need topic-collision detection that includes in-flight submissions, not just published articles.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. The earlier-merged PR #1193 covers this disclosure.
